message:
feat(scheduler): implement robust drift detection in benchmark_scheduler (bd-1765)

Add BenchmarkConditions struct to SpeedScore to record system conditions
(CPU%, memory%, load average) at benchmark time. The detect_drift()
method now compares current telemetry against these recorded conditions
using a weighted composite: CPU 50%, load average 30%, memory 20%.

Key design decisions:
- Only flags drift when conditions have WORSENED past the threshold,
  avoiding the old bug where idle workers (0% load) were incorrectly
  flagged as 100% drifted from a static 30% baseline
- Returns None for legacy scores without conditions (backwards compat)
- Returns None when current telemetry is unavailable
- BenchmarkConditions is Optional with serde skip_serializing_if/default
  for seamless JSON backwards compatibility

Includes 10 new tests covering:
- No conditions recorded (legacy)
- No current telemetry
- Conditions worsened significantly
- Conditions improved (no false positive)
- Conditions unchanged
- Idle-to-idle (the old bug scenario)
- Below-threshold small changes
- Serialization roundtrip
- Backwards-compatible deserialization

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
